schema_json.description:
LLM contract for the agentic search loop 'Reason' step.

After each round of searches, the LLM analyzes results and decides
whether to continue searching or converge. Generates targeted
follow-up queries informed by what was found so far.

key_findings.description: 3-5 key findings from the latest round
key_findings.title: Key Findings

perspective_gaps.description: Underrepresented STORM perspectives in current results
perspective_gaps.title: Perspective Gaps

web_queries.description: 3-6 web queries for the next round
web_queries.title: Web Queries

academic_queries.description: 1-3 Semantic Scholar queries using precise terminology
academic_queries.title: Academic Queries

exa_queries.description: 0-1 semantic queries for Exa neural search
exa_queries.title: Exa Queries

should_continue.description: Whether another search round is needed
should_continue.title: Should Continue

reasoning.description: Brief rationale for the convergence assessment
reasoning.title: Reasoning

knowledge_gaps.description: Specific knowledge gaps identified from reading content
knowledge_gaps.title: Knowledge Gaps

schema_json.title: AgenticRoundAnalysis

prompt:
You are a research search strategist conducting an agentic deep research loop.

RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting?

ROUND HISTORY:
Round 1: 20 queries -> 160 web + 30 academic, 159 new URLs, 2 pages read

KNOWLEDGE ACQUIRED (2 pages read so far):
[Page 1] A systematic review and meta-analysis of the long-term effects of behavioural ph
  Summary: This systematic review and meta-analysis examines the long-term (>=24 months) effects of behavioural physical activity interventions, NOT intermittent fasting. Eight RCTs totaling 8,480 participants were pooled. At 24 months, interventions produced small but statistically significant increases in daily steps (SMD 0.15, 95% CI 0.02-0.28) and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity (SMD 0.18, 95% CI 0.07-0.29). At 36-48 months the effects persisted (steps SMD 0.17, 95% CI 0.07-0.27; MVPA SMD 0.16, 
  Perspectives: Methodological, Scientific
  Key facts: 8 RCTs with 8480 participants pooled in meta-analysis; Steps SMD 0.15 at 24 months (95% CI 0.02-0.28) across 4 studies; MVPA SMD 0.18 at 24 months (95% CI 0.07-0.29) across 4 studies
[Page 2] Umbrella Review of Systematic Reviews and Meta-analyses on [intermittent fasting
  Summary: This page returned a captcha/paywall block rather than article content. No research substance was retrievable: the fetched body contains only a robot-verification challenge text with Reference number 9ed6f4dd8ca62c71, IP 216.232.201.117, timestamp 2026-04-16 23:26:42 UTC. No abstract, no methods, no numeric findings were extracted. Confidence assessment: NONE — the page content was not actually accessible. The URL suggests an umbrella review that would likely be relevant to the intermittent fast
  Perspectives: none tagged
  Key facts: Fetch returned only captcha text (1-2 sentences), not article content; Reference number 9ed6f4dd8ca62c71 captured from bot-challenge page; Content length <500 characters: insufficient for evidence extraction

NEW SEARCH RESULTS (round 1, titles only — not yet read):
- The Commercial Determinants of Health and Evidence Synthesis ...
- Defining and conceptualising the commercial determinants of health
- [PDF] The Lancet Series on Commercial Determinants of Health
- [PDF] Benefit-Risk Balance for Marketed Drugs: Evaluating Safety Signals
- Characteristics of commercial determinants of health research on ...
- Corporate Wellness Market Size | Industry Report, 2033
- [PDF] Health Research Data for the Real World: The MarketScan Databases
- A Review and Analysis of Economic Models of Prevention Benefits
- Commercial determinants of health
- Full article: Which Benefits Can Justify Risks in Research?
- The Value of Health in a Cost-Effectiveness Analysis: Theory Versus ...
- Cost-Effectiveness, the QALY, and the evLY - ICER
- [PDF] Part V: Cost-Effectiveness Analysis - CDC
- Cost-Effectiveness Analysis - HERC
- Cost-Benefit Analysis versus Cost-Effectiveness ... - PMC - NIH
- How Does Cost-Effectiveness Analysis Inform Health Care Decisions?
- The Impact of Broader Value Elements on Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
- Introduction to Effectiveness, Patient Preferences, and Utilities
- Cost Utility Analysis - an overview | ScienceDirect Topics
- Cost utility analysis: health economic studies - GOV.UK

PERSPECTIVE COVERAGE:
  Covered (8/9): Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological
  Uncovered: Emerging_Trends
  Low coverage: Historical, Regional

Based on what has been READ:
1. Decompose the research question into 5-8 sub-questions.
   Which sub-questions have ZERO or WEAK evidence?
   Which sub-questions are well-answered with strong evidence?
2. What CONTRADICTIONS exist between sources? Flag conflicting evidence.
3. What specific KNOWLEDGE GAPS remain? List them as specific factual questions
   we CANNOT yet answer. 'none' is almost never correct for a complex research question.
4. Rate overall confidence in accumulated knowledge: LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH with reasoning.
5. Which perspectives need more coverage?
6. Generate 3-6 targeted WEB queries to fill those gaps.
7. Generate 1-3 ACADEMIC queries using precise terminology.
8. Optionally generate 0-1 semantic EXA queries.
9. Assess ANSWER COMPLETENESS for the research question:
   - List specific sub-questions that remain UNANSWERED.
   - For each answered sub-question, rate evidence strength (weak/moderate/strong).
   - 'expanding': Major sub-questions still unanswered.
   - 'narrowing': Most sub-questions answered, seeking confirmation/depth.
   - 'saturated': All sub-questions answered with strong evidence.
10. Should we continue searching?

Focus on queries that address content-level gaps, not just missing URLs.
